subject: Physics
title: Cosmic Rays from Nowhere
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Cosmic Rays from Nowhere.

hook.text: Ultra-high-energy cosmic rays hit Earth with the energy of a baseball thrown at 100 km/h—packed into one proton.

buildup.text: Cosmic rays are charged particles—mostly protons—accelerated to near light speed by unknown cosmic engines.

discovery.text: The highest-energy cosmic ray ever detected—the Oh-My-God particle in 1991—had the energy of a bowling ball dropped on your toe.

twist.text: No known mechanism can accelerate a single proton to such energy. Their origin remains one of astrophysics' deepest puzzles.

climax.text: Active galactic nuclei and gamma-ray bursts are suspects, but definitive proof is still missing.

punchline.text: One proton with the energy of a speeding ball. From where? Nobody knows.

quiz.question: What is remarkable about ultra-high-energy cosmic rays?
options: A single proton carries macroscopic amounts of energy and their origin is unknown
They are made of dark matter particles
